paper_title: Topic based document modeling for information filtering

entities.description: The author of the research paper.

entities.id: School of Engineering and Technology, Tra Vinh University
entities.description: Academic institution located in Viet Nam where the author is affiliated.
The primary research field focused on document retrieval techniques for large-scale information.
A broader field encompassing information filtering and searching tools.
Latent Dirichlet Allocation with high Frequency Occurrences, the proposed topic-modelling model.
A topic-modelling technique used to model user interest.
A conventional baseline method representing document collections via term weights.
Probabilistic Latent Semantic Analysis used for automatic document indexing.
A baseline model that uses LDA-trained topics to represent user interest.
A statistical approach to measuring similarity and capturing latent structures.
Reuter Corpus Volume 1, a large benchmark dataset containing 806,791 stories.
A benchmark dataset widely used in text retrieval and categorization systems.
A measurement metric determining the relevance of the first 20 retrieved documents.
Break-even Point metric where precision and recall are equal.
Mean Average Precision, used to determine ranking algorithm effectiveness.
F-measure metric representing the harmonic mean of precision and recall.
The process of representing and capturing a user's long-term preferences.
Technique to model document collections in the form of topics with extracted words.

relations.source: Topic based document modeling for information filtering
